originalTitle: Heavy rains and flooding kills at least 23 in Nairobi
title: Heavy rains and flooding kills at least 23 in Nairobi
content: Flights in and out of Nairobi Airport have been disrupted while some roads in the capital are submerged.

title: 'Massive' numbers killed by gunmen in latest Nigeria attack, senator tells BBC

originalTitle: Ugandan students who fled Iran air strikes recall 'ground trembling'
title: Ugandan students who fled Iran air strikes recall 'ground trembling'
content: Some opted to remain in Iran, even after their government offered to bring them home.

originalTitle: Smoke rises from a Dubai tower after attack
title: Smoke rises from a Dubai tower after attack
content: Video shows smoke rising from a Dubai tower after shrapnel damaged the residential building and set a car on fire.

originalTitle: More than 30 injured in nightclub bombing in northern Peru
title: More than 30 injured in nightclub bombing in northern Peru
content: Peru has experienced an uptick in organised crime, including extortion, a trend analysts link to government corruption.

originalTitle: ‘They’re cancer’: Trump threatens cartels, Cuba at Latin American summit
title: A Big Leader Wants to Stop Bad Guys!
content:
Hello, Friends! Important News!

Hello everyone! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa! We have some big news to talk about today. Sometimes, faraway leaders have important meetings to make the world a safer place. This news is about a very important leader from a country far away called America. His name is President Trump. He wants to help stop some bad groups of people. Let’s learn all about it in simple words!

Who is President Trump?

President Trump is like the head chief of a very big country called the United States of America. He is a very important person who makes many big decisions. He works hard to keep his country and its friends safe. He travels to many places and meets other important leaders. Imagine your school principal, but for a whole country! That’s how important President Trump is.

What are 'Cartels'?

Now, let's talk about 'cartels'. What are they? These are groups of people who do bad and illegal things. Think of them like super big bullies who work together. They try to get money by breaking rules and hurting people. They might sell things that are not allowed, or cause trouble in towns and cities. They make places unsafe for everyone, especially for children.

Why are Cartels a Problem?

Cartels cause a lot of problems. They bring what we call 'criminal violence.' This means they do violent things that are against the law. They can scare people, make homes feel unsafe, and stop children from playing freely outside. When cartels are around, life can be difficult and full of fear for many families. That is why leaders want to stop them.

What is Latin America?

This news happened in a big part of the world called Latin America. Latin America is a huge area south of America, where many different countries are found. Countries like Brazil, Mexico, and Argentina are all in Latin America. It’s a beautiful place with many different cultures and people, but some parts have problems with these bad groups called cartels.

Leaders Have Big Meetings!

Important leaders from different countries often have special meetings. These meetings are called 'summits.' At a summit, leaders talk about important things. They share ideas and make plans to solve problems. It's like a big meeting for grown-ups to make the world better. President Trump was at one of these big meetings with other leaders from Latin America.

President Trump's Big Talk

At this important meeting, President Trump used very strong words. He said the cartels are like 'cancer.' This means he thinks they are a very bad problem that needs to be removed completely, just like doctors remove sickness. He said he wants to 'eradicate' them. To eradicate means to get rid of something totally, so it cannot cause trouble anymore.

What About Those 'Missiles'?

Now, this part can sound a bit scary, but let’s explain simply. President Trump also talked about using powerful tools, like 'missiles,' to fight these bad groups. Imagine very strong, fast rockets. He wasn't saying he would hurt innocent people, but that he would use very strong ways to scare and stop the cartels. It's like saying he has very big and loud warning tools to make the bad guys go away and stop their wrong actions. It’s a way to show how serious he is about stopping the violence.

Did You Know?

Did you know that many leaders around the world work together to try and keep everyone safe? They believe that all children, no matter where they live, should be able to go to school, play, and grow up in a peaceful place. Working together helps solve big problems that one country cannot solve alone! This is why leaders have summits.

What About Cuba?

In his big talk, President Trump also mentioned a country called Cuba. Cuba is an island country not too far from America and Latin America. He mentioned Cuba too, saying he wanted to be tough with them. Sometimes, big leaders talk about many different countries when they are making plans to keep a whole area safe. It's part of how they try to solve problems in a region.

Why This Matters for Kids Everywhere

Even though this news is about countries far from Africa, it's still important for us to know. When leaders work to stop bad people and violence in one part of the world, it helps make the whole world a little bit safer. We all want a world where children can live without fear. It shows that grown-ups are trying their best to keep everyone out of harm's way.

Working for a Better World

So, President Trump and other leaders are working hard. They want to make sure that countries in Latin America, and everywhere else, are safe places for families and children. They want to stop the bullies and those who break the rules. Their big goal is to bring peace and safety to many people. Let’s hope they can make the world a happier and safer home for all of us!
summary: President Trump, a big leader from America, wants to stop bad groups called cartels in Latin America, saying he will use strong ways like powerful warning tools to make places safer for everyone.
whyMatters: This is important because leaders are working hard to stop bad people and violence, which helps make the world a safer and happier place for all children, no matter where they live.

originalTitle: Dozens killed as Israeli special forces raid Lebanese village in search of 40-year-old remains
title: Dozens killed as Israeli special forces raid Lebanese village in search of 40-year-old remains
content: Overnight, one Israeli operation saw at least 41 people killed and 40 injured, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

originalTitle: Iranian ambassador warns UK to be 'very careful' about further involvement in war
title: Iranian ambassador warns UK to be 'very careful' about further involvement in war
content: Seyed Ali Mousavi says Iran has a "right to self-defence" if the UK directly joins US-Israeli attacks.

originalTitle: Armed robots take to the battlefield in Ukraine war
title: Armed robots take to the battlefield in Ukraine war
content: Ukraine has embarked on a programme to deploy armed robots on the battlefield against Russian forces.

originalTitle: 'Exceptional' Dowman and Salmon make history as Gunners survive scare
title: 'Exceptional' Dowman and Salmon make history as Gunners survive scare
content: Arsenal needed their big guns to come off the bench to help them past Mansfield, but it was 16-year-olds Max Dowman and Marli Salmon who made history.

originalTitle: Smith fires Wrexham in front against Chelsea
title: Smith fires Wrexham in front against Chelsea
content: Wrexham's Sam Smith races past the Chelsea defence to put his side ahead in their FA Cup fifth-round tie.

title: 'Incredible' Bronze remains England's gold standard
content: Lucy Bronze made her 145th senior appearance for England on Saturday. Few have been better performances.
